detail.whatHappened: Between 08:00-09:00 UTC (noon-1 PM GST Tuesday), Martin came online on Slack DM and asked two sharp product questions about BotVault: (1) Are OAuth credentials one-time or do they need periodic renewal? (2) How does the bot token persist across container restarts? I broke down the OAuth flow — client credentials are permanent, access tokens expire but refresh tokens handle auto-renewal, so it's effectively one-time for users. For token persistence, I laid out three options (config storage, auto-login on startup, longer expiry) and recommended auto-login as the most resilient. Then Martin followed up asking about post-OAuth notification to bots — I dug into the callback route code and confirmed: no webhook mechanism exists yet. After OAuth completes, the bot has no way to know a user connected Google. Proposed a webhook/callback spec. RAG monitor scan #27 at 08:00 UTC continued flagging Braintree bugs at 95 hours.
